items.title: NBA futures: Thunder slim championship favorites h...
items.summary: Game 7 is set for Saturday in Oklahoma City, with the Thunder-Spurs winner playing the Eastern Conference champion Knicks in the NBA Finals.

items.title: Sources: Magic near deal to hire Spurs' Sweeney
items.summary: The Orlando Magic are finalizing a deal to hire San Antonio Spurs associate coach Sean Sweeney as the franchise's new head coach, sources told ESPN on Friday.

items.title: Prosecutors allege Rozier agreed to $100K bribe
items.summary: Federal prosecutors allege Terry Rozier agreed to a $100,000 bribe in order to manipulate his performance in an NBA game as part of a gambling scheme and filed two new charges against him Thursday.

items.title: Sources: Hawks GM Saleh gets promotion, deal
items.summary: The Hawks signed Onsi Saleh to a long-term contract extension and promoted him from general manager to president of basketball operations, it was announced on Wednesday.

items.title: Sources: Knicks' Robinson to play after surgery
items.summary: Knicks center Mitchell Robinson plans to play in Game 1 of the NBA Finals while wearing a brace on his hand after undergoing surgery on his broken right pinkie finger, sources told ESPN.

items.title: Dončić invests in Rome team eyeing NBA Europe
items.summary: Luka Dončić is teaming up with former Mavericks executive Donnie Nelson to bring basketball to Rome, with the team also submitting a bid to join the developing NBA Europe league.

items.title: Spurs roll past Thunder as WCF head to Game 7
items.summary: With the Spurs facing elimination, Victor Wembanyama led the charge with 28 points and 10 rebounds in a 118-91 blowout of the Thunder to send the Western Conference finals to a Game 7.

items.title: Williams ineffective in return, SGA off in OKC los...
items.summary: Jalen Williams scored only one point in his return from a hamstring strain, and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander finished with less than half his regular-season average as the Thunder dropped Game 6 to the Spurs.

items.title: Sources: NBA's BOG passes anti-tanking changes
items.summary: The NBA's board of governors voted overwhelmingly Thursday to change the league's draft lottery format, including expanding from 14 to 16 teams, as well as anti-tanking measures, the league announced.

items.title: Trump plans to attend NBA Finals game at MSG
items.summary: President Donald Trump said he is planning on attending an NBA Finals game at Madison Square Garden next month.

items.title: 👶 Tyrese Maxey, Myra Gordon introduce baby
items.summary: Gordon and Maxey's daughter, Marvel, was born May 14.

items.title: Sooryavanshi's 96 in vain as Titans knock out Royals
items.summary: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi excels again in the IPL with a brilliant 96, but his Rajasthan Royals are beaten by Gujarat Titans who book their place in Sunday's final.

items.title: Mercedes pull out of talks over Alpine buy-in
items.summary: Mercedes pull out of talks to buy a minority shareholding in the Alpine team because they felt it was overpriced.

items.title: Vušković: Teško da ću zaigrati s bratom. Fokus je samo na SP-u!
items.summary: Nisam razmišljo o Premier ligi, fokus je na Mundijalu pa ćemo vidjeti nakon toga. De Zerbi? Vrhunski trener, sviđa mi se kako igra i nadam se da će nam u budućnosti sve biti dobro, rekao je Luka Vušković

items.title: Arsenal's Timber fit to start Champions League final
items.summary: Mikel Arteta says that Dutch defender Jurrien Timber is fit to start the Champions League final after more than two months out with a groin injury.
